result.markdown:
---
title: "The Multiverse"
subtitle: "Every line is a justifiable analysis"
engine: knitr
format:
  live-html:
    toc: true
    webr:
      packages:
        - ggplot2
resources:
  - data/spec_grid.csv
  - data/analysts5.csv
  - data/class_results.csv
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---


::: {.cell}

:::





This page shows the specification curve for the workshop exercise. The static charts below render at build time from the pre-computed menu; the **Class results** chart at the foot of the page is live – it fetches the room's submissions in your browser and redraws when you press *Run Code*. Before the session – or when the data files are absent – the page renders with placeholder messages.

The logic of the visualisation follows the specification-curve idea (Simonsohn, Simmons and Nelson): one point per justifiable analysis, ordered by size. Because the menu mixes outcome scales and exposure transforms, raw coefficients are not comparable across specifications – the chart therefore plots the **partial correlation** (r = t/sqrt(t^2 + df)), the same metric Multi100 used to compare analysts. A horizontal line at zero marks the boundary between negative and positive associations. Your contribution sits somewhere on this chart.

---

## Specification curve


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](results_files/figure-html/spec-curve-1.png){fig-alt='Specification curve: partial correlations for 1,680 specifications, ordered by size, coloured by outcome variable, with the constrained baseline marked. Horizontal line at zero.' width=864}
:::
:::


Across all 1680 specifications: **69%** point in the claim-consistent direction, **64%** are significant at the 5% level, and **59%** are both. The partial correlation ranges from -0.59 to 0.70. Same data, same claim – the answer depends on the path.

---

## Five Multi100 analysts (partial r)


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](results_files/figure-html/analysts-plot-1.png){fig-alt='Dot plot of partial r values for the five Multi100 analysts. Horizontal line at zero.' width=672}
:::
:::


---

## Class results – 16 June 2026

This chart is **live**. It draws the pre-computed menu (grey) and the five Multi100 analysts (the dashed lines, C6HJR in orange) as a backdrop, then overlays the room's own submissions as orange dots, placed where each result's partial *r* falls on the curve. Press **Run Code** to pull the latest submissions.

The R engine downloads into your browser the first time (10–30 seconds); the button changes from a spinner to *Run Code* when it is ready.


::: {.cell autorun='true' caption='Press Run Code to refresh – pull the latest class results'}
```{webr}
#| label: live-multiverse
#| autorun: true
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5
#| caption: "Press Run Code to refresh – pull the latest class results"
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CONFIG. Set worker_url to your Cloudflare Worker URL once it is deployed
# (see facilitator/live-results-setup.md, steps 1-3). Leave it as "" before the
# Worker exists: the chart still renders the backdrop + the five analysts, so the
# page works at any stage of setup.
worker_url <- "https://class-results.chris-moreh.workers.dev/"
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

library(ggplot2)
ncl_blue <- "#0e3b6a"; orange <- "#E69F00"; d_red <- "#a10000"; grey_mid <- "#848c94"

# Backdrop, from the page's bundled resources (always available, even with zero
# submissions or a failed fetch).
spec <- read.csv("data/spec_grid.csv")
spec <- spec[order(spec$r), ]
spec$rank <- seq_len(nrow(spec))
an <- read.csv("data/analysts5.csv")
an$me <- an$analyst_id == "C6HJR"

# Class results. Three regimes, in order of preference:
#   1. live  – fetch from the Worker (cache-busted so each Run pulls fresh);
#   2. archive – if the Worker is unset or fails, read a committed snapshot
#                (data/class_results.csv), written after the session;
#   3. none  – fall back to just the backdrop.
read_clean <- function(path) {
  d <- read.csv(path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)
  names(d) <- tolower(trimws(names(d)))   # tolerate "R", " r ", etc.
  d$r <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(d$r))
  d[!is.na(d$r), , drop = FALSE]
}
cls <- NULL
if (nzchar(worker_url)) {
  cls <- tryCatch({
    download.file(paste0(worker_url, "?_=", as.numeric(Sys.time())),
                  "class.csv", quiet = TRUE)
    read_clean("class.csv")
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}
if (is.null(cls) && file.exists("data/class_results.csv")) {
  cls <- tryCatch(read_clean("data/class_results.csv"), error = function(e) NULL)
}

p <- ggplot(spec, aes(rank, r)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = grey_mid, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(colour = "#c7ccd2", size = 0.7, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(data = an[!an$me, ], aes(yintercept = r),
             colour = grey_mid, linetype = "22", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(data = an[an$me, ], aes(yintercept = r),
             colour = orange, linetype = "22", linewidth = 0.9) +
  labs(x = "specification (ranked by partial r)", y = "partial r",
       title = "The class multiverse – live") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.75, 0.78)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", colour = ncl_blue),
        axis.title = element_text(colour = ncl_blue))

if (!is.null(cls) && nrow(cls) > 0) {
  cls$rank <- findInterval(cls$r, spec$r)
  p <- p +
    geom_point(data = cls, aes(rank, r), colour = orange, size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
    labs(subtitle = paste(nrow(cls), "class result(s) so far"))
} else {
  p <- p + labs(subtitle = "No submissions yet – press Run Code to refresh")
}
p
```
:::


::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## How this works (facilitator)

Submissions go to a Google Form, land in a linked Google Sheet, and are served as CSV with permissive CORS headers by a small Cloudflare Worker. The `{webr}` cell above fetches that CSV in the browser. Full setup and run-day steps are in `facilitator/live-results-setup.md`.
:::
